title: cymbalaria muralis subsp. pubescens
intro: Cymbalaria muralis subsp. pubescens, commonly known as the hairy or downy toadflax, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to exhibit potential in treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Cymbalaria muralis subsp. pubescens include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and showing promise in combating certain bacterial and fungal infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to leverage its medicinal properties.
